location: /resources/faq/[slug] + select /blogs/[slug] pages + select local landing pages
count: 29 FAQ pages + ~2-3 blog posts with faqs arrays
quality_notes: Quality varies — FAQ pages are well-structured question/answer format. Blog post FAQs mirror content from HTML sections. All use standard FAQPage/@type Question/@type Answer structure.
improvement_area: Add faqs arrays to more high-traffic Medicare comparison blog posts. Minimum target: all posts with question-format titles.

location: /resources/faq/* (all pages — titles are questions); select blog posts (which-medicare-plan-is-better-for-dental-and-vision-coverage etc.)
count: 29 FAQ pages with question titles; ~15 blog posts with question-format titles
quality_notes: Strong pattern — blog posts in the which-medicare-plan-is-better-for-* series are natively question-formatted. Resource FAQ pages all have question H1s.
improvement_area: Ensure H2 headings inside blog posts are also framed as questions where appropriate (not just H1/title).

location: /resources/faq/[slug] pages — keyTakeaways array; blog post FAQ sections
count: 29 FAQ pages have keyTakeaways (3-5 bullet summary per page)
quality_notes: keyTakeaways rendered as bullet list — AI models and featured snippet algorithms reward direct concise answers at top of content. Current pattern buries answers after introductory text.
improvement_area: Consider adding TL;DR/answer summary block at top of high-traffic blog posts before the full article body.

location: Person schema @graph; /about/steve; BlogPostingSchema author; all page metadata
quality_notes: Person schema with @id /#steve-germain cross-referenced from BlogPostingSchema author and LocalBusinessSchema founder/employee — correct entity linking for knowledge graph.
improvement_area: Add sameAs links to official license lookup (apps.fldfs.com) or NPN registry in Person schema to strengthen entity identity.

quality_notes: InsuranceAgency+LocalBusiness @id /#organization consistently used. sameAs includes Facebook Instagram YouTube TikTok LinkedIn GBP.
improvement_area: Add Wikipedia or Wikidata entry if possible. Add DUNS number or business registry identifier to schema.

location: Content throughout site; schema knowsAbout arrays
quality_notes: Medicare referenced as primary topic across all content. CMS and SSA cited per editorial policy. Medicare.gov linked in footer.
improvement_area: No formal schema sameAs link to Medicare.gov or CMS in LocalBusinessSchema. Consider adding cms.gov to knowsAbout or about entities.

count: 6 social profiles + GBP Google Maps link
quality_notes: sameAs includes Facebook Instagram YouTube TikTok LinkedIn GBP CID. No Wikidata/Wikipedia/official government registry identifiers.
improvement_area: Add NPN registry URL (apps.fldfs.com lookup for NPN 19921707) to Person sameAs. Consider GS1 or DUNS for Organization.

quality_notes: No Speakable schema implemented — this marks content sections ideal for voice assistant reading.
improvement_area: Implement Speakable on FAQ pages and key definition content sections. Google still supports this for voice search responses.

quality_notes: Calculator pages and enrollment checklist post are natural candidates for HowTo schema.
Distinct from FAQPage — used for pages with community Q&A format. Not currently applicable but worth considering for resources.
improvement_area: Low priority — FAQPage is the correct schema for current content structure.

quality_notes: Pillar guides provide detailed definitional content (What Is Medicare Advantage? What Is Medigap?) in H2 sections. Good pattern for AI entity extraction.
improvement_area: Add structured comparison tables (e.g. Medicare Advantage vs. Medigap feature comparison table) — tables are strong AI signal.

location: Blog post HTML content (Webflow-migrated) may contain tables; .blog-content CSS in globals.css includes table styles
count: Unknown count — content is raw HTML in strings
quality_notes: The blog-content CSS includes table styling suggesting some posts have comparison tables. Cannot confirm without rendering.
improvement_area: Audit top 10 posts for table presence. If absent add comparison tables to Medicare Advantage vs. Medigap posts where rankings are being competed for.

location: All service pages use 01 02 03... numbered item format; pillar guides have structured sections
count: 7 service/coverage pages + all local landing pages
quality_notes: Numbered item format on service pages is AI-readable structured content. Each item includes description and The Bottom Line callout.
improvement_area: Good pattern already established — extend to FAQ pages where applicable.

location: 7 pillar guides + 29 FAQ pages + ~35 Medicare blog posts
quality_notes: Site has a strong Medicare topical authority cluster. GSC confirms impressions across diverse Medicare queries.
improvement_area: Largest gap: no content about Medicare in languages other than English (despite Spanish knowsLanguage claim in schema).

location: 5 local landing pages + 3 local-oriented blog posts + LocalBusinessSchema areaServed (16 cities)
quality_notes: GSC shows homepage receiving health insurance agents miami fl at pos 18 (105 impressions / 0 clicks). Local SEO gap — not ranking for Miami Medicare searches.
improvement_area: Need more Miami/Florida-specific content. GSC data shows local queries getting impressions but very low positions.

location: No VideoObject schema found; YouTube channel exists (sameAs link) but no video embeds with schema in codebase
quality_notes: YouTube channel linked in sameAs but no VideoObject schema on any page. Workshop content at /watch/video is not schema-tagged.
improvement_area: Add VideoObject schema to /watch page for the Medicare Made Simple workshop. Embed YouTube videos on relevant blog posts with VideoObject schema.

location: keyTakeaways array in FAQ pages renders as bullet summary
quality_notes: FAQ pages have keyTakeaways rendered as bullets — good AI signal. Blog posts do NOT have equivalent summary structure.
improvement_area: Add structured summary section to top or bottom of high-traffic blog posts. Pattern: 3-5 key takeaways in a callout box.

location: Schema claims es as inLanguage but no Spanish content exists
quality_notes: WebSite schema lists inLanguage: [en-US es] but there is no /es/ or Spanish-language content in the site.
improvement_area: If Spanish content is planned add /es/ language route with hreflang. If not remove Spanish from inLanguage to avoid misleading Google about content availability.

signal_type: AI citation signals — external authoritative links
location: Medicare.gov linked in footer; CMS/SSA cited per CLAUDE.md editorial policy (in content)
count: 1 explicit external link in code (Medicare.gov in footer)
quality_notes: External links to authoritative sources in content make content citable by AI systems. CLAUDE.md states every dollar figure traces to CMS/SSA but external links within post content body cannot be confirmed from source code.
improvement_area: Audit top 10 posts for presence of external links to Medicare.gov CMS.gov SSA.gov. Add if missing.
